Discuss Area 4 — Per-role visibility & click behavior

human_actions_pending.action: Merge v3.3 → main + run `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules`
human_actions_pending.context: Carried over from Phase 87.4 — D-03/D-04 server-side attachment-presence gate not live in production until then

decisions.decision: 5 home tiles = Clients, Projects, Services, Procurement, Finance (1:1 with top-nav)
decisions.rationale: User picked Clients as the 5th; mirror top-nav rather than re-frame as broader department landings

decisions.decision: All tiles visible to every role; click denied falls through to existing router.js route protection
decisions.rationale: Simplest model; no new soft-disable state required

decisions.decision: Phase 87.1 sub-nav (Overview | Engagements | Proposals) retained as-is; Management stays in top nav
decisions.rationale: User explicitly chose 'remove management' from sub-nav; Management is not a tile or sub-nav entry

decisions.decision: Overview sub-tab restructured: 5 tiles on top, Procurement stats card below
decisions.rationale: User: 'No retain tiles in overview. Tiles first then stats'

decisions.decision: Tile visual = current .nav-card style; layout = 3-on-top + 2-centered-below; descriptions revised per current functionality
decisions.rationale: User: 'match the current layout of tiles i like it, and just revise their description to much they current functionality'

next_action: Run /gsd-plan-phase 93 to break Phase 93 into plans. Optionally run /gsd-ui-phase 93 first (UI hint: yes).
context_notes: Discuss-phase 93 complete in --batch mode (switched to default interactive mid-flow at user request). CONTEXT.md captures 10 decisions (D-01..D-10) covering 4 areas: department identity, sub-nav fate, tile layout, click behavior. Primary edit target for planning will be app/views/home.js (~650 lines), styles/hero.css (.nav-card + .navigation-cards), and possibly styles/views.css (no regression to .home-sub-nav from Phase 87.1). Pure UI rebrand — no firestore.rules edits, no new collection.
